title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - Prologue - Last Man
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use as critique of comfort-seeking and shrinking aspiration, not as contempt for ordinary human limits.
misreading_risk: Can be misread as glorifying cruelty or rejecting all rest.
raw_text:
The “last man” is Nietzsche’s image of a human type that prefers comfort, safety, ease, and small satisfaction over risk, striving, transformation, or greatness. The figure is not simply relaxed or peaceful. It represents a shrinking of aspiration, a refusal of higher possibility, and a way of life built around avoiding difficulty.

For the purposes of this project, this source should be used as a warning against making comfort into the highest ideal. It should not be used to mock ordinary exhaustion, recovery, or legitimate human limits.
